creator: hetzels@westbend.net
text:
From: <will@FreeBSD.org>
> Synopsis: New port variable USE_COMPAT_LIB - bsd.port.mk
>
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: will
> State-Changed-When: Sun Dec 23 02:50:42 PST 2001
> State-Changed-Why:
> Why should this be split off into another bsd.*.mk?  I guess the question
> would be met with "why should any of the others?" but could you provide
> a real answer here?
>
With it in a separate bsd.*.mk file, it wouldn't need portmgr approval when
another compat lib entry needs to be added for the next major release.

But, I'll leave it up to the portmgr team as to whether it belongs in
bsd.port.mk, or a separate bsd.*.mk.

Scot
